text:
### Reasons the respondent can use to dispute a divorce application

- Jurisdictional grounds - for example that neither of you have lived in England or Wales, which means the court doesn’t have the legal power or ‘jurisdiction’ to deal with the divorce.
- The validity of the marriage - this means that the marriage was never valid in the first place so that, in fact, you cannot be divorced.
- The marriage, or civil partnership, has already been legally ended.

If none of these apply in your situation the respondent cannot stop the application.

metadata.source: How to get a divorce without a lawyer: Advicenow
metadata.section: The next steps after the court has received the application
